data.title: CHANNELS: THE VISUAL VARIABLES
blocks: Position where on the x-axis, where on the y-axis
Length how long, how tall
Area how large the enclosing region is
Color hue what color (red vs. blue vs. green)
Color luminance how light or dark

data.title: WHY AREA GETS UNDERESTIMATED: STEVENS' POWER LAW
blocks: 1.7 times the original — not 2 times
1.5 times the original

How This Changes the Prompt You Write — When you ask Claude Code to "make a box plot of these AI capability scores," Claude Code will produce something that loo
data.section: HOW THIS CHANGES THE PROMPT YO

data.title: THE FEYNMAN TEST.
blocks: · What are the marks?
· What channels are those marks using to vary?
· What data attribute does each channel encode?
· For each quantitative attribute, is the channel a magnitude channel?
· For each categorical attribute, is the channel an identity channel?

data.title: EXERCISES
blocks: · A bubble chart where x-position is GDP per capita, y-position is life expectancy, bubble area is population, and bubble
· A choropleth map where polygon fill luminance encodes unemployment rate.
· A scatterplot where point shape (circle, square, triangle) encodes year (2020, 2021, 2022).

data.title: KEY TERMS
blocks: Mark.
Channel.
Magnitude channel.
Identity channel.
Expressiveness principle (Munzner).
